worth_it_explanation: IAS pre-bid suitability segments stop spend from reaching brand-unsafe or unsuitable inventory before a bid is placed. Post-bid monitoring records unsafe impressions after they are served and billed. The gap between those two modes is the gap between prevention and documentation. These checks confirm that the account has activated pre-bid segments in each DSP, that the segment configuration reflects the brand's actual suitability standards rather than a default, and that pre-bid and post-bid settings are aligned so the two modes measure and block the same content. The configuration audit takes place inside IAS Signal and inside each DSP's brand safety settings.
intro: Integral Ad Science (IAS) offers two distinct protection modes inside a DSP: post-bid monitoring, where IAS scores impressions after they are served and reports on brand safety, invalid traffic (IVT), and viewability in IAS Signal, and pre-bid avoidance, where IAS segment data is applied inside the DSP before a bid is placed so the bidder never competes for impressions that fall outside the brand's suitability thresholds. These two modes are not interchangeable, and activating one does not activate the other. IAS's Context Control product delivers pre-bid avoidance by providing the DSP with contextual segment IDs the account selects; the DSP excludes any impression matching those segments from the bidding process. These checks audit whether pre-bid segments are applied in each DSP, whether the segment selection matches the brand's post-bid suitability profile, and whether the protection is applied evenly across campaigns rather than only on selected line items.

audit_checks.title: IAS pre-bid suitability segments are not applied in the DSP, so the account pays for inventory IAS would have blocked
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without pre-bid segments, the DSP bids on all available inventory, including inventory that IAS classifies as brand-unsafe or unsuitable. Post-bid monitoring in IAS Signal documents the exposure, but the impressions are already purchased and billed. Every unsafe impression that post-bid reporting flags represents spend that pre-bid avoidance would have stopped before the bid entered the auction.

educate.base:
Integral Ad Science (IAS) delivers brand safety protection at two points in the programmatic buying process. Post-bid monitoring is enabled when the DSP sends impression data to IAS after the auction resolves and the ad is served. IAS scores each impression for brand safety, suitability, and IVT, and those scores appear in IAS Signal. Post-bid monitoring tells an account what ran and how it was classified, but the impression has already been purchased and billed before the classification is made.

Pre-bid avoidance operates differently. IAS provides participating DSPs with contextual segment IDs, generated by IAS's Context Control product, that identify inventory falling into specific content categories or risk levels. Before placing a bid, the DSP checks the impression against the segment list and excludes it from the auction if it matches a restricted segment. The advertiser never pays for the flagged impression because the DSP never competed for it.

The two modes require separate activation steps. A DSP integration that sends post-bid impression data to IAS Signal does not automatically apply any pre-bid segment. Each DSP has its own process for selecting and applying IAS pre-bid segments; in DV360, for example, pre-bid IAS Context Control segments are entered directly in the line item's brand suitability section under third-party verification. In Quantcast, they are selected in the ad set's brand safety section. A DSP that has post-bid reporting active but no pre-bid segment selected is watching but not blocking.

A few signs suggest pre-bid segments may not be active despite IAS being connected:
  • IAS Signal shows post-bid brand safety or IVT violations on the account, but DSP campaign settings show no IAS segment IDs entered in the brand safety or third-party verification section
  • The post-bid violation rate in IAS Signal has not changed after the DSP's IAS integration was set up, which would be expected if pre-bid avoidance had started filtering impressions before they reached the post-bid measurement stage
  • The DSP account settings show IAS listed as a measurement partner or tag provider, but no Context Control segment IDs appear under brand safety or content avoidance targeting
You may want to review brand safety targeting settings directly in each DSP rather than relying on IAS Signal data to determine whether pre-bid segments are applied. Signal reflects only what has been served; it does not surface what the DSP is configured to avoid.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For ecommerce advertisers running display or video campaigns across DSPs, brand-unsafe placements carry both reputational risk and a direct cost in wasted spend. You may want to confirm that IAS Context Control pre-bid segments are active in each DSP so that unsuitable inventory is excluded before the bid, rather than documented in IAS Signal after the impression is served and the budget has been spent.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For lead-generation advertisers where brand adjacency to harmful or low-quality content can affect the quality of leads and the credibility of the offer, you may want to confirm that IAS pre-bid segments are applied in each active DSP so that brand safety protection operates before the bid rather than only in post-bid reporting.

learn_more.source_title: About Integral Ad Science's media quality verification in Display and Video 360
Enabling Pre-Bid IAS Segments in an Ad Set

educate.learn_more_note: IAS's own documentation on Context Control pre-bid segment setup within specific DSPs is available through the IAS help center at helpcenter.integralplatform.com, which requires a logged-in IAS account. The segment IDs and the process for entering them into a DSP's brand safety settings vary by platform; each DSP's help documentation describes the specific steps for activating third-party pre-bid segments.

investigate.intro: Confirm, for each active DSP in use, whether IAS Context Control pre-bid segment IDs are entered in the brand safety or third-party verification settings, or whether the IAS integration is post-bid monitoring only.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: Log in to IAS Signal and pull a post-bid brand safety report for the past 30 days, broken down by DSP or buying platform. Note the brand safety violation rate and IVT rate for each platform. These figures represent what was served and classified after the impression was purchased.

Note whether the violation rates indicate a material share of impressions reaching brand-unsafe or unsuitable inventory. This establishes the scale of exposure that pre-bid avoidance would address if applied.

In IAS Signal, navigate to the pre-bid segment or Context Control section if accessible in your account tier. Confirm whether pre-bid segments are listed as configured for each active DSP, or whether only post-bid monitoring appears.

dsp_ui.label: DSP brand safety settings
steps: In each active DSP, navigate to the brand safety, third-party verification, or content targeting section at the campaign or account level.
For DV360: open the line item targeting and locate the brand suitability section under 'Other verification services'. Confirm whether IAS Context Control segment IDs are entered there.
For Quantcast: open the ad set's Inventory section, then the Brand Safety subsection. Confirm whether IAS pre-bid segments are selected, with risk levels or segment IDs specified.
For any other DSP in use, locate the equivalent brand safety or third-party data section and confirm whether IAS segment IDs are applied or whether IAS appears only as a measurement tag.
Record, for each DSP, whether pre-bid segments are active or whether only post-bid monitoring is in place. Note the campaign and insertion order names for any DSP where pre-bid is absent.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each active DSP, whether IAS Context Control pre-bid segment IDs are entered in the brand safety settings. A DSP with confirmed pre-bid segments applied passes. A DSP where IAS is present only as a post-bid measurement tag, with no pre-bid segment IDs in the brand safety or targeting settings, is a fail recorded with the DSP name and the post-bid brand safety violation rate from IAS Signal for that platform.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each active DSP and whether IAS pre-bid segments are applied or absent above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that IAS pre-bid suitability segments are not applied in one or more of the DSPs used by {{site_name}}. The IAS integration is present and generating post-bid brand safety data in IAS Signal, but the DSP is not configured to avoid the flagged inventory before the bid is placed. Every brand safety or IVT violation in the post-bid report represents an impression that was purchased and billed before IAS classified it as unsuitable. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the brand safety or third-party verification settings in each active DSP and entering the appropriate IAS Context Control segment IDs so that pre-bid avoidance is applied alongside post-bid monitoring, stopping spend from reaching inventory the account has already identified as outside its suitability standards.

audit_checks.title: The IAS pre-bid segment selection does not match the post-bid suitability profile, so pre-bid avoidance and post-bid reporting measure against different standards
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When pre-bid and post-bid settings are configured independently, the DSP may avoid categories that post-bid monitoring does not flag as violations, or vice versa. The result is a reporting picture that does not reflect the avoidance that is actually in place. Budget is either over-restricted relative to what the brand's post-bid standards call for, or the post-bid report surfaces violations that pre-bid should have caught but did not. The two configurations diverged.

educate.base:
IAS pre-bid segments and post-bid monitoring are configured independently in most DSP setups. Post-bid settings are established within IAS Signal, where the account defines which content categories are flagged as violations for reporting purposes. Pre-bid segments are applied separately within each DSP, where the account selects IAS Context Control segment IDs and risk levels to exclude from bidding. Because the two configurations happen in different interfaces, they can drift apart.

A campaign team may set conservative post-bid monitoring thresholds in IAS Signal to flag all moderate-risk content as a violation, then select only high-risk pre-bid segments in the DSP to limit the impact on available inventory. The result is that post-bid reporting shows moderate-risk impressions as violations, but pre-bid avoidance was never configured to block them. The report looks like the DSP failed to filter content that it was actually never instructed to avoid.

The reverse scenario also occurs. A DSP may have aggressive pre-bid segments applied at the account level, excluding a wide set of content categories, while the post-bid suitability thresholds in IAS Signal are set at a moderate level. Post-bid reports show low violation rates, which appear to confirm strong performance, but the pre-bid restriction is reducing eligible inventory beyond what the brand's actual suitability standards require.

IAS offers a Quality Sync pre-bid segment within certain DSP integrations specifically to address this configuration gap. Quality Sync reads the advertiser's post-bid avoidance settings from IAS Signal and translates them into a single pre-bid segment within the DSP, so the two modes are calibrated to the same standard. Where Quality Sync is not available or not activated, the pre-bid and post-bid configurations must be reviewed and aligned manually.

A few signs the two configurations may have diverged:
  • IAS Signal shows post-bid brand safety violations in categories that the DSP's pre-bid segments are supposed to be blocking, which could indicate that the pre-bid segment IDs do not include those categories
  • Pre-bid segment IDs in the DSP cover a wider range of content categories than the brand's documented suitability policy identifies as restricted
  • The account went through a platform migration or agency transition and the pre-bid settings were transferred from a prior setup without being verified against the current post-bid thresholds in IAS Signal
You may want to compare the specific content categories covered by the active pre-bid segment IDs in the DSP against the content categories flagged as violations in IAS Signal's post-bid configuration before concluding the two are aligned.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For ecommerce advertisers where news and politics adjacency may not be a concern but adult content and fraud are, you may want to confirm that the pre-bid segment selection covers the content categories the brand's suitability policy restricts without over-excluding categories that produce acceptable and converting traffic.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For lead-generation advertisers running campaigns with a broad audience scope, an over-restricted pre-bid segment that excludes content categories the post-bid standard does not flag as violations will reduce the eligible impression pool and raise cost per lead without improving the quality of the inventory that does reach the campaign.

learn_more.source_title: Brand suitability in Display and Video 360

educate.learn_more_note: IAS's Quality Sync pre-bid segment, which mirrors post-bid suitability settings from IAS Signal into a single unified pre-bid segment within a DSP, is documented in the IAS help center at helpcenter.integralplatform.com, which requires a logged-in IAS account. Availability of Quality Sync varies by DSP integration.

investigate.intro: Compare the content categories covered by the active IAS pre-bid segment IDs in the DSP against the content categories configured as violations in IAS Signal's post-bid reporting settings.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: In IAS Signal, navigate to the brand safety and suitability settings for the relevant advertiser. Note which content categories are flagged as violations at which risk levels, for example, adult content at high risk, news and politics at moderate risk, and so on.

Pull a post-bid brand safety report for the past 30 days. Identify which content categories are appearing in the violation breakdown and at what volume. These categories represent what post-bid monitoring is classifying as outside the brand's suitability standard.
Note the list of categories and risk thresholds the post-bid configuration is set to flag. This becomes the reference standard for comparing against the DSP's pre-bid configuration.

dsp_ui.label: DSP brand safety settings
steps: In each active DSP, open the brand safety or third-party verification settings and locate the IAS pre-bid configuration.

Record which IAS Context Control segment IDs are applied and, where the DSP provides category labels alongside the IDs, which content categories they correspond to. In DV360, the brand suitability section lists the segment IDs and the category names. In Quantcast, the brand safety section lists risk levels by content category.
Compare the content categories covered by the pre-bid segment IDs against the categories flagged as violations in IAS Signal's post-bid configuration from the previous step.
Identify categories where the two configurations diverge: categories flagged post-bid but not excluded pre-bid, and categories excluded pre-bid that are not flagged in the post-bid configuration.

Check whether the DSP integration supports Quality Sync pre-bid segments. If it does, confirm whether Quality Sync is activated. A Quality Sync segment automatically reflects the post-bid settings from IAS Signal, which would resolve the alignment gap without requiring manual comparison.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the content categories covered by the active IAS pre-bid segment IDs in each DSP and compares them against the content categories flagged in IAS Signal's post-bid configuration. A DSP where the pre-bid categories and risk levels match the post-bid configuration, or where Quality Sync is active and confirmed to be syncing correctly, passes. A DSP where material categories are present in one configuration but absent in the other is a fail recorded with the DSP name, the categories that diverge, and whether the divergence represents under-restriction or over-restriction relative to the post-bid standard.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the pre-bid segment categories from the DSP and the post-bid violation categories from IAS Signal above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that the IAS pre-bid segment configuration in the DSP does not match the suitability thresholds set for post-bid monitoring in IAS Signal for {{site_name}}'s campaigns. The two configurations were set up independently and have drifted apart, so the content categories the DSP is excluding before the bid do not correspond to the categories IAS Signal is flagging as violations after delivery. This creates a reporting picture that either overstates the protection in place or restricts inventory beyond what the brand's documented suitability standards require. {{voice_recommend}} comparing the content categories and risk levels in the active IAS pre-bid segment IDs against the post-bid violation configuration in IAS Signal, resolving the discrepancies so the two modes reflect the same suitability standard, and confirming whether Quality Sync pre-bid segments are available in each active DSP integration as a way to keep the configurations aligned automatically.

audit_checks.title: IAS pre-bid suitability segments are applied on some campaigns but absent on others, leaving certain line items without brand safety controls
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Line items running without pre-bid segments bid on the full available inventory pool, including inventory that other campaigns on the same account are actively excluding. The brand's suitability standard applies inconsistently depending on which campaign or line item delivers the impression. Post-bid reporting from IAS Signal shows violation rates at the campaign level, but does not automatically surface which line items lack pre-bid coverage.

educate.base:
In most DSPs, IAS pre-bid segments are applied at the campaign, insertion order, or line item level rather than at a global account level. The scope of the setting depends on how the DSP structures its brand safety controls. In DV360, for example, third-party verification segments including IAS Context Control IDs are applied at the line item level and must be configured on each line item individually, unless inherited from insertion order defaults. A new line item created without copying from a templated setup may launch without any pre-bid segments applied, even if other line items in the same account have them.

This creates a coverage pattern where pre-bid protection is present on campaigns that were set up by a team member familiar with the IAS configuration, and absent on campaigns that were built at different times, by different teams, or under time pressure. The account's brand safety standard varies by line item origin rather than by a deliberate policy decision.

The gap is not visible in IAS Signal without a line-item-level breakdown. A campaign-level post-bid report that shows an acceptable brand safety violation rate may mask a single line item without pre-bid segments that is responsible for the remaining violations. Signal's aggregate view makes it easy to conclude the account is protected when specific line items within it are not.

A few patterns suggest uneven coverage may be present:
  • The account has campaigns that predate the IAS integration, which were never updated to include pre-bid segments after the integration was set up
  • Different agencies or traders manage different campaigns for the same advertiser without a shared brand safety configuration standard
  • Line items have been duplicated or trafficked from a creative asset without inheriting the brand safety settings from the source campaign
You may want to review pre-bid segment configuration at the line item level, not only at the campaign or account level, before concluding coverage is complete.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For ecommerce advertisers running a mix of prospecting and retargeting campaigns, retargeting line items are often set up separately and may not carry the same brand safety configuration as prospecting campaigns. You may want to confirm pre-bid segments are active on retargeting line items as well. The audience reached there includes people who have already interacted with the brand.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For lead-generation advertisers managing multiple campaigns across different audience segments or geographies, line items built for smaller or lower-priority audiences may have been configured without the same level of attention to brand safety settings as the primary campaigns. You may want to verify pre-bid coverage extends to all active line items regardless of budget size or campaign priority.

learn_more.source_title: About Integral Ad Science's media quality verification in Display and Video 360

educate.learn_more_note: IAS Signal documentation on pre-bid segment reporting and coverage visibility is available through the IAS help center at helpcenter.integralplatform.com, which requires a logged-in IAS account.

investigate.intro: Review pre-bid segment configuration at the campaign and line item level in each active DSP to identify any active line items that are missing IAS pre-bid segments.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: In IAS Signal, pull a post-bid brand safety and IVT report for the past 30 days, broken down by campaign and, if the account tier supports it, by line item. Note which campaigns or line items show the highest brand safety violation or IVT rates.

Compare violation rates across campaigns. A significant spread in violation rates across campaigns on the same account, using the same DSP and targeting similar audiences, may indicate that higher-violation campaigns are missing pre-bid segments that lower-violation campaigns have applied.
Note any campaigns where the brand safety violation rate is substantially higher than the account average. These are candidates for a pre-bid configuration review in the DSP.

dsp_ui.label: DSP brand safety settings
steps: In each active DSP, open the brand safety or third-party verification settings at the campaign and line item level. Do not review only the account-level defaults; confirm which settings are actually applied on each active line item.

For DV360: open each active insertion order and navigate to its line items. For each line item, open the targeting settings and confirm whether IAS Context Control segment IDs appear in the brand suitability or third-party verification section.
For Quantcast: open each active ad set's brand safety settings and confirm whether IAS pre-bid segments are selected and which risk levels are applied.
Record, for each active line item or ad set, whether pre-bid segments are present or absent. Prioritize line items that receive the largest share of the campaign budget.

For line items where pre-bid segments are absent, note whether the account-level or insertion-order-level default settings would pass through a pre-bid configuration to line items automatically, or whether each line item requires independent configuration.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each active line item reviewed, whether IAS pre-bid segments are applied. A line item with pre-bid segments confirmed in the DSP settings passes. A line item with no pre-bid segment configuration is a fail, recorded with the campaign name, the insertion order, the line item name, and the approximate share of campaign spend it represents. The practitioner notes whether the gap is an isolated instance or a pattern affecting multiple campaigns.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each active line item and whether IAS pre-bid segments are applied or absent above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that IAS pre-bid suitability segments are applied on some of {{site_name}}'s active line items but absent on others within the same account and DSP. The campaigns with pre-bid segments are configured to avoid brand-unsafe and unsuitable inventory before the bid is placed; the campaigns without them bid on the full inventory pool, including content that the account's IAS suitability settings identify as unsuitable. The brand's protection standard varies by line item rather than applying consistently across the account. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing pre-bid segment configuration at the line item level across each active DSP, applying the appropriate IAS Context Control segment IDs to all line items that are currently missing them, and establishing a trafficker checklist or campaign template that includes pre-bid segment configuration as a required step so future line items launch with coverage in place.
